turns.user_message: I’ve finally accepted that I’m just a quiet observer in everyone else’s story. This used to not bother me at all, but lately it’s sitting heavy on my shoulders. I keep noticing how my thoughts get brushed aside during casual conversations, and I just let it slide. It feels like I’m always the one listening while everyone else takes up all the air in the room. I guess I’m just tired of being the easygoing one who never complains.

I keep catching myself shrinking back whenever things get loud, even though I used to jump right into the middle of the action. It’s becoming this exhausting routine where I wait for an opening that never actually shows up. I watch my own thoughts dissolve before I can even share them, and it leaves this weird hollow feeling in my chest. The worst part is realizing I’ve trained myself to expect being talked over. I’m starting to resent how easily I just fade into the wallpaper.

I’ve completely convinced myself that my voice simply doesn’t carry any real weight anymore. What started as a minor annoyance has turned into a constant, gnawing frustration that follows me everywhere I go. I catch myself rehearsing sentences in my head just to abandon them halfway through, terrified of taking up space. It’s exhausting to feel so thoroughly invisible while pretending I don’t notice the gap. I just wish I could remember how to take up room without feeling like I’m stealing it.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a conclusion they've reached about themselves. This used to not bother the speaker.
